decisions.title: Client-side TDMS parsing vs server-side migration strategy
decisions.context: ENG-9980 implements client-side TDMS parsing for detect_configs while ENG-7822 migrates to npTDMS server-side. Need to decide on unified approach to avoid dual maintenance burden and ensure consistent performance across ingestion paths.

arguments.for: Client-side parsing reduces server load and enables faster preview; aligns with Edge strategy of pushing compute to client
arguments.against: Creates inconsistent parsing logic across system; client-side limits ability to handle large TDMS files; complicates security model

decisions.title: Family stats minimum run requirement default
decisions.context: ENG-7853 adds support for minimum input runs, but no product decision on default value. This affects Canvas usability and customer trust in statistical validity. Related to North Star metric as it impacts decision confidence.

arguments.for: Default to 3+ runs ensures statistical validity and prevents misleading single-run 'families'; builds customer trust
arguments.against: May create friction for early-stage testing workflows where customers have <3 runs; could hide useful comparisons

decisions.title: Flow channel caching architecture: LRU vs disk-based priority
decisions.context: ENG-8937 adds in-memory LRU cache, ENG-8950 adds disk cache. Need to decide eviction policy and cache hierarchy to prevent thrashing on high-channel-count workloads. Impacts Explore V2 performance at scale.
decisions.blockedBy: Performance testing results on >10k channel workloads not yet available

arguments.for: Two-tier caching (memory LRU + disk persistence) provides best latency while supporting large datasets; proven pattern
arguments.against: Complexity of cache coherency between tiers; potential for stale data if invalidation logic is imperfect; adds debugging surface area

decisions.title: FedRAMP compliance timeline acceleration
decisions.context: ENG-9684 in progress but no clear gate for 'FedRAMP-ready'. Government customer pipeline requires certification by Q3 2026. Session timeout (ENG-9304) and error handling (ENG-9920) are compliance prerequisites. Moat pillar depends on this for gov sector expansion.

arguments.for: Accelerate to Q2 certification to unlock $2M+ gov pipeline; demonstrates enterprise commitment; competitive moat
arguments.against: Rushing certification risks audit failure; engineering capacity is constrained; may deprioritize core product work

decisions.title: Playwright/Bullfinch e2e testing investment scope
decisions.context: ENG-9972 begins bootstrapping selectors. Need to decide coverage target: critical paths only vs comprehensive regression suite. Impacts velocity on Canvas and Explore V2 iterations. Build the Moat pillar benefits from quality but Nail the Core requires speed.

arguments.for: Comprehensive suite prevents regressions as we ship Canvas/Explore V2 features rapidly; pays back in reduced customer issues
arguments.against: High maintenance burden slows feature velocity; critical path coverage (authentication, data load, annotation creation) may be sufficient for now

decisions.title: Annotation visibility bug prioritization vs new features
decisions.context: ENG-9861 (annotations invisible) is a production bug affecting customer trust in Canvas. Competes with new features like preset panel configs (ENG-9884) and family stats UI (ENG-7856). Nail the Core pillar requires stability, but roadmap has aggressive feature commitments.

arguments.for: Fix immediately - customers use annotations for critical decisions; broken trust damages North Star metric and retention
arguments.against: Workaround exists via Chart View; new features drive expansion revenue and competitive positioning

keyResults.name: Canvas family stats adoption >40% of customers
Python Rules deployed in production at 5+ customers

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load due to FedRAMP compliance planning, Canvas roadmap review, and architecture decisions on caching. Focus blocks limited - recommend blocking Friday AM for decision documentation.
alerts: Three open architecture decisions without owners scheduled to discuss
FedRAMP decision deadline (Apr 7) conflicts with customer exec review
Annotation bug fix decision needs resolution before sprint planning Monday
Backend capacity at 105% - caching work may slip without reprioritization
